output.final_deliverable:
1) Exact H1 text from https://example.com:
   - H1 (exact): "Example Domain"
   - Source (visited page): https://example.com

2) Exact domain of the external link from https://example.com:
   - External link (full URL found on page): https://iana.org/domains/example
   - Domain (exact): iana.org
   - Source (visited page): https://example.com

3) One URL citation for each visited page:
   1. https://example.com
   2. https://www.iana.org/domains/reserved

Notes:
- All values above are taken from the provided visit results for each page (h1_text, links, and source_url fields).
output.action_summary: Visited https://example.com and https://www.iana.org/domains/reserved. URL citations returned: https://example.com and https://www.iana.org/domains/reserved.

details.intent: After both visits complete, validate extracted values and produce the final structured response the user asked for: (1) exact H1 text from example.com, (2) exact domain of the external link from example.com, and (3) one URL citation for each visited page.
inputs_expected_from_children: From visit https://example.com: h1_text_exact, external_link_url_exact, external_link_domain_exact, page_url_citation
verification_steps: Confirm H1 text is non-empty and return it verbatim.
Confirm external_link_domain_exact is the domain parsed from external_link_url_exact (exact substring of href); if mismatch, return both the href and the domain and note discrepancy.
Confirm page_url_citation values are the exact URLs to be returned as citations (use the exact strings captured during visit).

final_output_format.external_link_domain: string (exact domain from the external link on example.com)

c8335fe4-cfbc-41fa-b23f-96d625ae89ab.title: Visit https://example.com and extract H1 and external link domain

details.intent: Fetch the exact H1 text from the page, locate the first external link on the page, and produce one URL citation for this visited page.
target_facts: h1_text_exact: the exact text content of the first <h1> element on https://example.com
external_link_url_exact: the exact href of the first external (non-example.com) anchor on the page
external_link_domain_exact: the exact domain portion of that external link (e.g., example.org)
page_url_citation: the canonical URL to cite for this visited page (should be https://example.com or a canonical link found on the page)
details.extraction_plan: Load https://example.com. Extract the first <h1> element text verbatim (including punctuation and whitespace exactly as in source). Find anchor (<a>) tags and identify the first href whose domain is not example.com (i.e., an external link). Record the exact href string and parse only the domain portion (preserve exact subdomain if present). Record the visited page's URL (use the exact URL fetched or canonical link if present). Store the raw snippets for verification.
verification_steps: Include the HTML snippet around the extracted H1 to show context.
Include the exact anchor snippet where the external link was found to prove the domain extraction.
Confirm the extracted domain is the domain substring from the exact external_link_url_exact.
details.rationale: The user explicitly asked for the exact H1 text and the exact domain of the external link on example.com; visiting the exact URL is required to capture verbatim text and link URLs.
details.goal: Visit https://example.com and extract H1 and external link domain

details.intent: Fetch the page and produce one URL citation for this visited page; capture a top-level heading/title for verification.
target_facts: page_url_citation: the canonical or exact visited URL to cite for this page
page_h1_or_title_exact: the exact H1 or <title> text for verification (if present)
details.extraction_plan: Load the provided URL. Extract the page URL used for citation (use the exact requested URL or canonical URL if present). Extract the first H1 on the page; if no H1 exists, extract the document <title> text. Store a small snippet showing the location for verification.
verification_steps: Include the small HTML/text snippet where the H1 (or title) was extracted for evidence.
Return the exact URL string used as the citation (no normalization).
details.rationale: The user requested one URL citation for each visited page; visiting the exact IANA reserved URL ensures an authoritative citation and provides a verification heading to confirm the page visited.
